ops.title: Base-model sweep results 2026-07-18: qwen3.5-9b wins on the uniform Tinker-frame instrument
ops.body:
Three-arm baseline sweep executed 2026-07-18 (gated ~$49, orchestrator go; per-arm caps 18/18/30 all held; workdirs ~/.cc-steer/sweep/2026-07-18/<arm>/, E8-winner recipe with base ids swapped per arm, `cc-steer score-watcher` direct invocations):

| arm | Tinker-frame sentinel AUC | serves_locally | projected cost |
|---|---|---|---|
| **qwen3.5-9b** | **0.9371** | false | $27.37 |
| qwen3-8b (incumbent) | 0.9301 | true | $8.02 |
| qwen3.5-4b | 0.9262 | false | $13.78 |

Reading: 9B leads the incumbent by +0.0070 AUC under the identical recipe; 4B trails by −0.0039 and is dominated (worse AND non-local) — drop it. Single seeded run per arm, no CI — the 9B lead is real on this measurement but promotion-grade confidence wants repeats or the served/judged confirmation path.

Decision: **the deployed watcher stays on qwen3-8b for now** (serves locally, free at inference; the served-MLX gate remains its promotion instrument). **qwen3.5-9b is the tracked upside candidate**: it becomes deployable either when athome ships an mlx-lm LoRA counterpart for it (then the normal retrain/promotion path confirms under the served gate) or via a hosted-serving decision (Tinker sampler — note the operator's in-flight `TinkerBackend.sample` + `sampler_path` work in athome Unreleased, which is exactly the serving primitive a hosted 9B watcher would need). Also: 9B inference via Tinker bills $0.44/$1.33 per Mtok — the cost/quality tradeoff vs a free local 8B is the operator's call.

Note the arc: the user's correction ("no one said it _has_ to run locally") is what surfaced this result — the serves_locally admission filter would have run a one-arm sweep and never measured the winner. Supersede context: [[2e933ab1]] (the un-deferral decision note).
